title: The k Strongest Values in an Array
titleCn: 数组中的 k 个最强值

testcases.input: arr = [1,2,3,4,5], k = 2 Output: [5,1] Explanation: Centre is 3, the elements of the array sorted by the strongest are [5,1,4,2,3]. The strongest 2 elements are [5, 1]. [1, 5] is also accepted answer. Please note that although |5 - 3| == |1 - 3| but 5 is stronger than 1 because 5 > 1.
testcases.expectedOutput: [5,1] Explanation: Centre is 3, the elements of the array sorted by the strongest are [5,1,4,2,3]. The strongest 2 elements are [5, 1]. [1, 5] is also accepted answer. Please note that although |5 - 3| == |1 - 3| but 5 is stronger than 1 because 5 > 1.

testcases.input: arr = [1,1,3,5,5], k = 2 Output: [5,5] Explanation: Centre is 3, the elements of the array sorted by the strongest are [5,5,1,1,3]. The strongest 2 elements are [5, 5].
testcases.expectedOutput: [5,5] Explanation: Centre is 3, the elements of the array sorted by the strongest are [5,5,1,1,3]. The strongest 2 elements are [5, 5].

testcases.input: arr = [6,7,11,7,6,8], k = 5 Output: [11,8,6,6,7] Explanation: Centre is 7, the elements of the array sorted by the strongest are [11,8,6,6,7,7]. Any permutation of [11,8,6,6,7] is accepted.
testcases.expectedOutput: [11,8,6,6,7] Explanation: Centre is 7, the elements of the array sorted by the strongest are [11,8,6,6,7,7]. Any permutation of [11,8,6,6,7] is accepted.
